# Playbook

## Purpose

- Organize content by the **problem it solves**, not by type.
- Optimize for fast discovery and real-world use.

## Curation Rules

- **Preserve links**: Never remove existing resources.
- **Categorize by problem**: Place each link where it’s most useful.
- **One category only**: Choose the most practical fit.
- **Write good descriptions**: Clear, practical, and opinionated (max 110 characters).
- **Always tag types**: Add a type tag at the end of every link.

## Link Format (Required)

- Format:

`- [Resource Name](https://url) - Short practical description *(type)`*

- Example:

`- [Flexbox Froggy](https://flexboxfroggy.com) - Learn Flexbox alignment through an interactive game *(playground)*`

## Allowed Type Tags

> Tags are ordered by priority. When in doubt, choose the **first applicable tag from top to bottom**.

### English | Portuguese

- `(generator)` | `(gerador)`  
  - Creates something automatically (borders, gradients, etc.)
- `(framework)` | `(framework)`  
  - Provides a structured system to build interfaces
- `(course)` | `(curso)`  
  - Structured learning content with lessons or modules
- `(video)` | `(vídeo)`  
  - Video content explaining a concept or technique
- `(podcast)` | `(podcast)`  
  - Audio discussion or interview about CSS topics
- `(preprocessor)` | `(pré-processador)`  
  - Extends CSS with features like variables and nesting
- `(playground)` | `(playground)`  
  - Interactive environment to test and experiment
- `(tool)` | `(ferramenta)`  
  - Utility that helps build, debug, or improve CSS
- `(methodology)` | `(metodologia)`  
  - Approach or system for organizing and writing CSS
- `(library)` | `(biblioteca)`  
  - Reusable collection of styles or components
- `(guide)` | `(guia)`  
  - Step-by-step explanation of a topic
- `(article)` | `(artigo)`  
  - Written content explaining concepts or ideas
- `(reference)` | `(referência)`  
  - Lookup-style resource for quick consultation
